headline: 5 ERC-20 wrapped token contracts catalogued on Base (+ Ethereum and Arbitrum for cbBTC); no on-chain reads completed due to domain access blocks on DeFiPunkd and block explorers.

findings.text: D1: Basescan.org was not directly fetchable (permission denied); search-grounded snippets from basescan.org confirmed cbBTC at 0xcbB7C0000aB88B473b1f5aFd9ef808440eed33Bf and cbDOGE at 0xcbd06e5a2b0c65597161de254aa074e489deb510 on Base. The contract name for cbBTC is 'FiatTokenProxy' (Circle template). Native chains listed in protocol.chains (Bitcoin, Ripple, Doge, Cardano, Litecoin) have no EVM explorers; these are the collateral-custody chains, not EVM deployment chains.

D2: coinbase.com and help.coinbase.com were inaccessible via direct fetch (permission denied). Search-grounded snippet from coinbase.com/blog confirmed official cbBTC contract addresses on Base, Ethereum, Arbitrum, and Solana. coinbase.com/campaigns/wrapped-assets lists Proof of Reserves pages for all five wrapped assets but does not enumerate contract addresses in the snippet. No canonical deployed-contracts doc URL was confirmed via successful fetch; set as unknown.

D3: No audit links were pinned in protocol.audit_links. Search results indicate the new tokens (cbDOGE, cbXRP, cbADA, cbLTC) use the same smart contract template 'audited for cbETH', but no audit PDF URL was surfaced or fetchable in this run.
D4: No GitHub repo is pinned (protocol.github = none). No Foundry broadcast artifacts or hardhat-deploy files were discoverable. No deployment manifest was found.

D5: protocol.chains lists Bitcoin, Ripple, Doge, Cardano, Litecoin — these are non-EVM custody chains with no EVM contract addresses. The EVM deployments (Base, Ethereum, Arbitrum) are the issuance side of the bridge. cbBTC is confirmed on Base, Ethereum, and Arbitrum (all same address 0xcbB7C0000aB88B473b1f5aFd9ef808440eed33Bf). Other wrapped tokens (cbDOGE, cbXRP, cbADA, cbLTC) appear Base-only based on available evidence.
D6: No factory contract was identified. Each wrapped asset is a standalone FiatTokenProxy (Circle's token framework). No enumeration view methods applicable.

D7: All five addresses classified as role=token, actor_class=unknown (proxy admin / owner roles not readable in this run due to access blocks). Contract name FiatTokenProxy (Circle framework) suggests an admin/owner role pattern but on-chain reads were blocked.

D8: All five addresses trace to fetched search-result snippets from basescan.org, coincentral.com, and coinbase.com/blog. No address was sourced from memory alone. DeFiPunkd read-API was inaccessible (permission denied on domain). Admin/owner addresses behind each proxy are unknown pending on-chain reads.

rationale.verdict: Walked coinbase.com, help.coinbase.com (blocked), coinbase.com/blog (search snippet), basescan.org (search snippet), coincentral.com (search snippet), and altcoinbuzz.io (fetched). Catalogued 5 ERC-20 token contracts (cbBTC on Base/Ethereum/Arbitrum, cbDOGE/cbXRP/cbADA/cbLTC on Base) from Coinbase's official announcements. On-chain reads for proxy admin/owner roles were blocked; those addresses remain to be discovered by the next run.

evidence.shows: Official Coinbase blog post lists cbBTC contract addresses: Base and Ethereum both 0xcbB7C0000aB88B473b1f5aFd9ef808440eed33Bf, Arbitrum 0xcbB7C0000aB88B473b1f5aFd9ef808440eed33Bf, Solana cbbtcf3aa214zXHbiAZQwf4122FBYbraNdFqgw4iMij. Confirmed ERC-20 backed 1:1 by BTC held by Coinbase.
Basescan confirms cbBTC (Coinbase Wrapped BTC) ERC-20 token at this address on Base, contract name FiatTokenProxy, compiler v0.6.12.
Basescan confirms cbDOGE (Coinbase Wrapped DOGE) ERC-20 token at this address on Base, backed 1:1 by DOGE in Coinbase custody. Uses Circle Apache-2.0 licensed contract code.

Citing Coinbase Assets official X account, confirms cbXRP Base contract address 0xcb585250f852C6c6bf90434AB21A00f02833a4af, cbADA 0xcbADA732173e39521CDBE8bf59a6Dc85A9fc7b8c, cbLTC 0xcb17C9Db87B595717C857a08468793f5bAb6445F — all Base (chainId 8453).

Fetched article confirms Coinbase Assets (@CoinbaseAssets) announced official Base contract addresses on May 13, 2025 to prevent scam token confusion. cbADA: 0xcbADA732173e39521CDBE8bf59a6Dc85A9fc7b8c confirmed; cbDOGE address was truncated in this source.

Confirms Coinbase adopted Chainlink CCIP as the sole cross-chain bridge for its wrapped asset suite (cbBTC, cbXRP, cbDOGE, cbADA, cbLTC), replacing bespoke per-chain bridges. This is the cross-chain transport layer for the bridge product.

Confirms cbADA and cbLTC launched on Base on June 25-26 2025 as ERC-20 tokens backed 1:1 by assets in Coinbase custody, with proof-of-reserves pages published. New tokens use same smart contract template audited for cbETH.
Official Coinbase page listing all five wrapped assets (cbBTC, cbXRP, cbDOGE, cbADA, cbLTC) and their Proof of Reserves pages. Confirms wrapping/unwrapping is custodial via Coinbase.

unknowns: D1: Basescan.org direct fetch blocked (permission denied); address confirmation relies on search-grounded snippets only — on-chain state (owner, admin, paused) not read
D1: cbBTC Ethereum deployment (0xcbB7C0000aB88B473b1f5aFd9ef808440eed33Bf on chainId=1) not verified via direct on-chain fetch this run
D1: cbBTC Arbitrum deployment (0xcbB7C0000aB88B473b1f5aFd9ef808440eed33Bf on chainId=42161) not verified via direct on-chain fetch this run
D2: No canonical deployed-contracts documentation page URL confirmed via successful fetch; coinbase.com/campaigns/wrapped-assets is a candidate but did not expose contract addresses in snippets
D3: Audit report PDF URL not found — search results mention cbETH audit as template baseline but no PDF link was surfaced
D4: No GitHub repo pinned; no deployment artifacts discoverable without GitHub source

D5: Non-EVM chains (Bitcoin, Ripple/XRP-Ledger, Dogecoin, Cardano, Litecoin) have no EVM contract addresses; Coinbase custody wallets on those chains are off-chain and not discoverable via EVM explorers
D6: No factory contract identified; each token is a standalone FiatTokenProxy deployment
D7: Proxy admin / owner addresses for FiatTokenProxy contracts not read — DeFiPunkd API access blocked (permission denied on domain) and Basescan direct fetch blocked

D8: cbDOGE address (0xcbd06e5a2b0c65597161de254aa074e489deb510) confirmed only via Basescan search snippet, not via direct fetch or DeFiPunkd API read — promote to admin_addresses but flag for re-verification

D8: cbXRP (0xcb585250f852C6c6bf90434AB21A00f02833a4af), cbADA (0xcbADA732173e39521CDBE8bf59a6Dc85A9fc7b8c), cbLTC (0xcb17C9Db87B595717C857a08468793f5bAb6445F) confirmed via secondary news source citing official @CoinbaseAssets X post — no direct block explorer fetch completed

protocol_metadata.about: Coinbase Bridge is Coinbase's suite of custodial wrapped assets that allows holders of non-EVM cryptocurrencies (Bitcoin, Dogecoin, XRP, Cardano, Litecoin) to use their assets within Ethereum-compatible DeFi ecosystems. Users send native assets to Coinbase custody, which mints equivalent ERC-20 tokens (cbBTC, cbDOGE, cbXRP, cbADA, cbLTC) 1:1 on Base and other EVM chains. Each token uses Circle's FiatTokenProxy contract template, and cross-chain transfers between EVM chains are routed through Chainlink CCIP. Redemption is custodial: users deposit wrapped tokens back to Coinbase to receive the underlying native asset.
